crates/mimir-core/src/catalog/mod.rs.name: action
crates/mimir-core/src/catalog/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Hand-written extraction types for deserializing 5etools JSON data.
These types focus on the fields we need for indexing, searching, and display.

# Design Principles

1. **Extraction-focused**: Only define fields we actually use
2. **Permissive deserialization**: Use `Option<T>` liberally since 5etools data varies
3. **JSON blobs for complex content**: Store `entries` as `serde_json::Value`
4. **Polymorphic with `#[serde(untagged)]`**: Handle variant formats gracefully

# Schema Reference

These types are based on the 5etools JSON schemas in `schema/5etools/`.
See <https://github.com/TheGiddyLimit/5etools-utils/tree/master/schema/brew-fast>

crates/mimir-core/src/dal/campaign/modul.name: reorder_module
crates/mimir-core/src/dal/campaign/modul.signature:
(
    conn: &mut SqliteConnection,
    campaign_id: &str,
    module_id: &str,
    new_position: i32,
) -> QueryResult<()>
crates/mimir-core/src/dal/campaign/modul.doc_comment:
Reorder a module by moving it to a new position (1-indexed).

Uses a sentinel value (-1) to work around the UNIQUE(campaign_id, module_number) constraint,
then shifts affected modules and places the target module at the new position.

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: MIGRATIONS

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: configure_connection
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Configure a connection with pragmas for optimal operation.

Enables:
- `journal_mode=WAL` for concurrent reads
- `foreign_keys=ON` for referential integrity
- `synchronous=NORMAL` for better performance with WAL

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: run_migrations
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.signature: (conn: &mut SqliteConnection) -> Result<Vec<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>>
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Run all pending migrations on the given connection.

Returns the list of migration names that were run.

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: init_database
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Establish a database connection, run migrations, and configure pragmas.

This is used during application startup to ensure the database is ready.
For on-demand connections after startup, use `create_connection`.

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: create_connection
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new database connection with pragmas configured.

Use this for on-demand connections after the database has been initialized.
Each connection is configured with WAL mode, foreign keys, and optimal settings.

crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.name: test_connection
crates/mimir-core/src/db.rs.doc_comment: Create an in-memory SQLite connection for testing.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.name: TAG_REGEX
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.doc_comment:
Regex to match 5etools tag markers like {@spell fireball|PHB}
Captures the display text (first word after the tag type, before any pipe)

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.name: flatten_entries
flatten_entry
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.doc_comment:
Flattens a single 5etools entry value into searchable plain text.

Use this when you have a single entry rather than an array.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.name: flatten_entry_recursive
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.doc_comment:
Recursively flattens an entry value into text fragments.
Tags like {@spell fireball|PHB} are preserved for render-time transformation.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.name: strip_5etools_tags
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/entry_flattene.doc_comment:
Strips 5etools tag markers like {@spell fireball} -> "fireball".

Common tags include:
- {@spell fireball} -> fireball
- {@creature goblin|MM} -> goblin
- {@item longsword|PHB} -> longsword
- {@condition blinded} -> blinded
- {@dice 1d6} -> 1d6
- {@damage 2d6} -> 2d6
- {@hit +5} -> +5
- {@dc 15} -> 15

# Arguments

* `s` - String potentially containing 5etools tags

# Returns

String with all tags replaced by their display text.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment: A search result from the FTS table.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.name: index_entity
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.signature:
(
    conn: &mut SqliteConnection,
    entity_type: &str,
    entity_id: i32,
    content_type: ContentType,
    name: &str,
    text_content: &str,
) -> QueryResult<usize>
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment:
Index a catalog entity in the FTS table.

# Arguments

* `conn` - Database connection
* `entity_type` - Type of entity (e.g., "monster", "spell")
* `entity_id` - Primary key of the entity
* `content_type` - Whether this is rules or fluff content
* `name` - Entity name
* `text_content` - Flattened text content to index

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.name: remove_entity_from_index
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.signature:
(
    conn: &mut SqliteConnection,
    entity_type: &str,
    entity_id: i32,
) -> QueryResult<usize>
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment:
Remove all FTS entries for an entity.

# Arguments

* `conn` - Database connection
* `entity_type` - Type of entity
* `entity_id` - Primary key of the entity

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.name: clear_entity_type_from_index
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment:
Remove all FTS entries of a specific type.

# Arguments

* `conn` - Database connection
* `entity_type` - Type of entity to remove

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.name: clear_index
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment: Clear the entire FTS index.

crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.name: search
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.signature:
(
    conn: &mut SqliteConnection,
    query: &str,
    limit: i32,
) -> QueryResult<Vec<FtsSearchResult>>
crates/mimir-core/src/fts/search.rs.doc_comment:
Search the catalog using full-text search.

# Arguments

* `conn` - Database connection
* `query` - FTS5 search query (supports boolean operators, phrases, etc.)
* `limit` - Maximum number of results

# Returns

Vector of search results ordered by relevance.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/collector.r.name: collect_source_entities
crates/mimir-core/src/import/collector.r.doc_comment:
Collect all entities from a source (book).

This is the main entry point for collecting content from a specific book
or source code.

# Arguments
* `repo_path` - Path to the 5etools data directory
* `source` - Source code to collect (e.g., "PHB", "DMG")

# Example
```ignore
let entities = collect_source_entities(&fivetools_path, "PHB")?;
println!("Found {} monsters", entities.count("monster"));
```

crates/mimir-core/src/import/collector.r.name: collect_classes_from_directory
crates/mimir-core/src/import/collector.r.doc_comment:
Collect classes, subclasses, and their features from the class directory.

Classes are stored as one file per class (e.g., class-fighter.json),
not one file per source. Each class file contains the class with its source,
along with classFeature and subclassFeature arrays.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.name: file_exists_for_source
crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.doc_comment:
Check if a data file exists for a given source.

Checks for files matching the pattern `data/{dir}/{prefix}-{source}.json`.

# Arguments
* `repo_path` - Path to the 5etools data directory
* `dir` - Subdirectory name (e.g., "bestiary", "spells")
* `prefix` - File prefix (e.g., "bestiary", "spells")
* `source` - Source code (e.g., "PHB")

crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.name: load_json_file
crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.doc_comment: Load and parse a JSON file.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.name: get_matching_files
crates/mimir-core/src/import/discovery.r.doc_comment:
Get all file paths matching a source pattern.

Finds files in the following patterns:
- Direct: `{prefix}-{source}.json`
- Fluff: `fluff-{prefix}-{source}.json`
- Sub-files: `{prefix}-{source}-*.json` (for adventures with multiple parts)

# Arguments
* `repo_path` - Path to the 5etools data directory
* `dir` - Subdirectory name (e.g., "bestiary", "spells")
* `prefix` - File prefix (e.g., "bestiary", "spells")
* `source` - Source code (e.g., "PHB")

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: DEFAULT_ALLOWED_GROUPS
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Default groups to include when importing.

Groups in 5etools:
- "core" - PHB, DMG, MM
- "supplement" - XGE, TCE, MPMM, etc.
- "setting" - Eberron, Ravnica, Theros, etc.
- "adventure" - Published adventures (CoS, LMoP, etc.)
- "screen" - DM Screen supplements
- "homebrew" - Homebrew content
- Other one-offs

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: new
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new import service with a database connection.

By default, only imports reference material (core, supplement, setting groups).
Use `with_all_groups()` to import everything including adventures.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: with_all_groups
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Import all groups (including adventures, screens, etc.)

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: with_groups
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Set specific groups to import.

Common groups: "core", "supplement", "setting", "adventure", "screen"

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: with_image_copy
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.signature: (mut self, source_img_dir: PathBuf, dest_img_dir: PathBuf) -> Self
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Configure image copying from source to destination.

All images from the source directory will be copied to the destination,
preserving the directory structure so paths in imported data work as-is.

- `source_img_dir`: Path to 5etools img directory
- `dest_img_dir`: Path to destination directory for images

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: import_from_directory
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Import all available sources from a 5etools data directory.

Each source is imported in its own transaction. If a source fails,
it's rolled back and the next source is attempted.

If image copying is configured (via `with_image_copy`), all images are
copied first before entity import begins.

Import all sources from a tar.gz archive, streaming directly without extraction.

This reads the tarball once, collecting all JSON files into memory,
then processes them without writing to disk.

then expands each variant against matching base items.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: expand_and_import_magic_variants
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Expand magic variant templates against base items and import the resulting concrete items.

For each Generic Variant (e.g., "+1 Weapon"), matches it against base items using
`requires`/`excludes` rules, then creates concrete items (e.g., "+1 Shortsword")
by merging the base item data with the variant's `inherits` block.

Extract CR from a monster entity.
CR can be a string directly or an object with a "cr" field.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: extract_creature_type
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Extract creature type from a monster entity.
Type can be a string directly or an object with a "type" field.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: extract_size
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Extract size from a monster entity.
Size is typically an array, we take the first element.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: extract_ritual
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Extract ritual flag from a spell entity.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: extract_concentration
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Extract concentration flag from a spell entity.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: rewrite_book_image_paths
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Rewrite image paths in book content to use local asset paths.

5etools book content contains image references with paths like "img/book/PHB/foo.webp".
This function processes the entire book content recursively and updates these paths
to work with the local image directory.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: extract_attunement_classes
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Extract class names from an attunement requirement string.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: base_item_matches_field
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Check if a base item field matches a requirement key-value pair.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: base_item_excluded
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Check if a base item should be excluded based on the variant's excludes block.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: build_expanded_item
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Build an expanded item JSON by merging base item data with variant inherits.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: expand_damage_type
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment: Map 5etools damage type codes to human-readable names for use in prose text.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: resolve_template_variables
crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve 5etools template variables ({=fieldName}) throughout a JSON object.
Replaces {=key} with the string value of that key in the same object.
Damage type codes are expanded to full names in prose context.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/service.rs.name: resolve_value_templates

crates/mimir-core/src/import/srd.rs.name: check_srd_status
crates/mimir-core/src/import/srd.rs.doc_comment:
Check the SRD status of an item.

Checks in order:
1. `srd` field: `true` → Include, `false` → Exclude, `"string"` → Include renamed
2. `basicRules` field: `true` → Include
3. Default: Exclude

Process a single item for SRD inclusion.

Returns `Some(SrdItem)` if the item is SRD content, `None` otherwise.

crates/mimir-core/src/import/srd.rs.name: transform_item_for_srd
crates/mimir-core/src/import/srd.rs.doc_comment:
Transform an item for SRD inclusion.

- If renaming, updates the `name` field and stores original in `originalName`
- Sets `source` to "SRD"
- Sets `srd` to `true`
- Removes `basicRules` field (non-OGC metadata)

crates/mimir-core/src/models/catalog/mon.doc_comment:
Extract indexed fields from a JSON value.

This parses the 5etools monster JSON and extracts:
- `cr` from `data.cr` (handles both string and object formats)
- `creature_type` from `data.type.type` or `data.type` (string)
- `size` from `data.size[0]`

crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..name: resolve_clone_data
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..signature:
(
    user_data: Option<&str>,
    cloned_from_name: Option<&str>,
    cloned_from_source: Option<&str>,
    lookup_fn: F,
) -> ServiceResult<String>
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..doc_comment:
Resolve the data blob for a homebrew entity, handling clone-from-catalog.

- If cloning (`cloned_from_name` + `cloned_from_source` provided): look up the
catalog entity's data, then deep-merge any user overrides on top.
- If not cloning: `user_data` is required and used as-is.

The `lookup_fn` fetches the catalog entity's data string given (name, source).

crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..name: deep_merge
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..doc_comment:
Deep merge `source` into `target`. For objects, recursively merge keys.
For all other types (arrays, strings, numbers), source overwrites target.

crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..name: validate_json
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..doc_comment: Validate that a string is valid JSON.

crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..name: enrich_monster_data
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..signature:
(
    data: &str,
    cr: Option<&str>,
    creature_type: Option<&str>,
    size: Option<&str>,
) -> ServiceResult<String>
crates/mimir-core/src/services/homebrew..doc_comment:
Ensure the monster data JSON blob includes cr, type, and size fields
that match the top-level metadata. The UI stat block renderer reads
these from the data blob, so they must be present for proper display.

crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.name: get_campaign_template
crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Get a campaign template by document type.

# Arguments
* `doc_type` - The document type identifier (e.g., "campaign_pitch", "world_primer")

# Returns
The template content if found, or `None` if the document type is unknown.

crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.name: get_module_template
crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Get a module template by module type.

# Arguments
* `module_type` - The module type (e.g., "general", "mystery", "dungeon")

# Returns
The template content if found, or `None` if the module type is unknown.

crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.name: get_play_notes_template
crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Get the play notes template.

Play notes are used for session-by-session DM notes within a module.

crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.name: list_campaign_templates
crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.doc_comment:
List all campaign templates with their types and titles.

# Returns
A vector of (doc_type, title) pairs for all campaign templates.

crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.name: list_module_templates
crates/mimir-core/src/templates/mod.rs.doc_comment:
List all module templates with their types and titles.

# Returns
A vector of (module_type, title) pairs for all module templates.

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: slugify
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment:
Slugify a monster name for use in filesystem paths.

Converts "Adult Red Dragon" to "adult-red-dragon".

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: token_relative_path
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment:
Generate the relative token path for storage in the database.

Returns a path like "tokens/MM/adult-red-dragon.png".

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: resolve_token_path
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment: Resolve a relative token path to an absolute filesystem path.

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: find_source_token
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment:
Find a token image in the 5etools image directory.

Searches for PNG and WEBP formats.
Returns the source path and extension if found.

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: copy_token
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment:
Copy a token image from the source directory to the app data directory.

Returns the relative path stored in the database, or None if no token was found.

crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.name: copy_tokens_batch
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.signature:
(
    img_dir: &Path,
    app_data_dir: &Path,
    monsters: &[(String, String)], // (source, name) pairs
) -> (usize, Vec<String>)
crates/mimir-core/src/tokens.rs.doc_comment:
Batch copy tokens for multiple monsters.

Returns the count of tokens successfully copied.

crates/mimir-core/tests/common/mod.rs.name: setup_srd_db
crates/mimir-core/tests/common/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Set up an in-memory database seeded with SRD fixture data.

This includes:
- PHB catalog source
- All 12 SRD classes
- All 12 SRD subclasses
- 170 class features
- 89 subclass features
- 7 backgrounds
- 17 races
- ~44 items
- ~43 spells
- ~17 monsters

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: Contour
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: A contour polyline — a sequence of 2D points.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: find_contours
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment:
Extract contour lines from a noise map at the given threshold.

Uses the Marching Squares algorithm to find edges where the noise
field crosses the threshold value.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: merge_contours
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment:
Merge contour fragments that share endpoints.

The tracer often produces many small fragments that are actually one ring.
This merges them by matching start/end points within a small tolerance.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: filter_contours
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Filter contours by minimum length (number of points).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: smooth_contours
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Smooth contours by averaging each point with its neighbors.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: smooth_polyline
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment:
Extracts contour polylines from 2D scalar fields at configurable thresholds.

Contour extraction via Marching Squares.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: cell_case
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.signature: (noise_map: &NoiseMap, x: usize, y: usize, threshold: f64) -> u8
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Compute the Marching Squares case index (0-15) for a cell.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: interpolate
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.signature: (v1: f64, v2: f64, threshold: f64) -> f64
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Interpolate position along an edge between two values crossing the threshold.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: cell_segments
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.signature:
(
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    x: usize,
    y: usize,
    threshold: f64,
) -> Vec<((f64, f64), (f64, f64))>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment:
Get the edge crossing point(s) for a given marching squares case.
Returns pairs of (entry_point, exit_point) as edge segments.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: trace_contour
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.signature:
(
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    visited: &mut [Vec<bool>],
    start_x: usize,
    start_y: usize,
    threshold: f64,
    w: usize,
    h: usize,
) -> Option<Contour>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Trace a contour starting from the given cell.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: next_cell
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.signature:
(
    cx: usize,
    cy: usize,
    exit: (f64, f64),
    w: usize,
    h: usize,
) -> Option<(usize, usize)>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment: Determine the next cell to visit based on the exit point.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.name: tests
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/contour.rs.doc_comment:
Extracts contour polylines from 2D scalar fields at configurable thresholds.

Contour extraction via Marching Squares.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/curves.rs.name: cubic_bezier
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/curves.rs.doc_comment:
Evaluate a cubic Bezier curve at parameter `t` (0.0–1.0).

Given 4 control points (p0, p1, p2, p3), returns the point on the curve at `t`.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/curves.rs.name: bezier_smooth
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/curves.rs.doc_comment:
Smooth a polyline using cubic Bezier interpolation.

Converts a sequence of waypoints into a smooth curve by generating
control points and evaluating the Bezier at `density` intermediate
points per segment.

# Arguments
* `points` - Input waypoints
* `density` - Number of interpolated points per segment (higher = smoother)

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/curves.rs.name: offset_polyline

Returns a new polyline offset by `distance` to the left (positive)

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.doc_comment:
Poisson Disc sampler using Bridson's algorithm.

Generates a set of points where no two are closer than `min_distance`.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.name: PoissonDisc

Poisson Disc sampling (Bridson's algorithm) with noise-gated variants.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.name: sample
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.signature:
(
        width: f64,
        height: f64,
        min_distance: f64,
        rng: &mut impl Rng,
        k: u32,
    ) -> Vec<Point>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.doc_comment:
Generate uniformly distributed points with minimum spacing.

# Arguments
* `width` - Area width
* `height` - Area height
* `min_distance` - Minimum distance between any two points
* `rng` - Random number generator (for reproducibility)
* `k` - Number of candidate attempts per active point (default 30)

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.name: sample_gated
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.signature:
(
        noise_map: &NoiseMap,
        lower: f64,
        upper: f64,
        min_distance: f64,
        rng: &mut impl Rng,
        probability: f64,
    ) -> Vec<Point>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/distribution.rs.doc_comment:
Generate noise-gated points — only places points where the noise value
at that position falls within `[lower, upper]`.

# Arguments
* `noise_map` - 2D noise field to gate against
* `lower` - Minimum noise value (0.0–1.0) to accept a point
* `upper` - Maximum noise value (0.0–1.0) to accept a point
* `min_distance` - Minimum spacing between points
* `rng` - Random number generator
* `probability` - Chance (0.0–1.0) of keeping each valid point

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/lib.rs.name: assets
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
`mimir-mapgen` generates `.dungeondraft_map` files from YAML configuration,
using procedural generation algorithms (noise, Poisson Disc sampling,
Marching Squares, Bezier curves) to create natural-looking outdoor maps.

This crate is standalone — it has no dependency on `mimir-core` or any
database layer.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.name: apply_island_mode
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.doc_comment:
Apply island mode: push values at edges toward 1.0 (high = water for terrain).
This creates a landmass in the center with water around the edges.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.name: apply_lake_mode
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.doc_comment:
Apply lake mode: push values at center toward 1.0 (high = water for terrain).
This creates water in the center with land around the edges.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.name: apply_radial_falloff
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.doc_comment:
Shared radial falloff. When `invert` is false, edges get pushed up (island).
When `invert` is true, center gets pushed up (lake).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.name: apply_canyon_mode
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.doc_comment: Apply canyon mode: force values below each threshold down to create sharp level boundaries.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/noise_gen.rs.name: to_byte_map

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/objects.rs.name: place_objects
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/objects.rs.signature:
(
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    config: &ObjectConfig,
    pixel_width: f64,
    pixel_height: f64,
    alloc: &NodeIdAllocator,
    rng: &mut impl Rng,
) -> Vec<MapObject>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/objects.rs.doc_comment:
Place simple objects using noise-gated Poisson Disc sampling.

# Arguments
* `noise_map` - Noise field for gating placement
* `config` - Object placement configuration
* `pixel_width` - Map width in pixels (grid_squares * 256)
* `pixel_height` - Map height in pixels
* `alloc` - Node ID allocator
* `rng` - Seeded random number generator

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/objects.rs.name: clear_corridor
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/objects.rs.doc_comment:
Remove objects that fall within a corridor (e.g., road clearing).

# Arguments
* `objects` - Mutable list of objects to filter
* `corridor` - List of center points defining the corridor
* `half_width` - Half-width of the corridor in pixels

(
    start: (f64, f64),
    target: (f64, f64),
    pixel_width: f64,
    pixel_height: f64,
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    path_width: f64,
    step_distance: f64,
    rng: &mut impl Rng,
    exclusion_zones: &[ExclusionZone],
) -> Vec<(f64, f64)>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/paths.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a meandering centerline using sinusoidal displacement + noise.

The path is generated **longer than the map** (extended beyond both edges),
then callers clip the result to the map rectangle. This means the sine wave
is at full amplitude when it crosses the map boundary, producing clean
entry/exit with no edge artifacts.

) -> Vec<(f64, f64)>
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/paths.rs.doc_comment:

For paths that enter and exit the rectangle once.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/paths.rs.doc_comment:
Greedy pathfinding walk from start toward target.

At each step, evaluates candidate positions within an FOV cone,
scores them by a weighted combination of noise value and progress
toward target, and picks the best.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/pipeline.rs.name: generate
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/pipeline.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a complete `.dungeondraft_map` from a config.

Runs the full pipeline:
0. Room layout (walls, portals, exclusion zones)
1. Generate noise map
2. Generate terrain (splat map) + room terrain overrides
3. Generate roads and rivers
4. Place objects (trees, clutter, clumps)
5. Clear object corridors around roads/rivers
6. Filter objects from room exclusion zones
7. Generate water bodies
8. Generate elevation contours
9. Apply lighting
10. Assemble walls/portals into Level
11. Return DungeondraftMap

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: collect_edges
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Extract directed edges from all polygons (in pixel coordinates).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: subtract_overlap
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Two segments overlap if they are collinear and share any interval.
Returns the non-overlapping remainders of segment `a` after subtracting
the overlap with segment `b`. Returns None if no overlap.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: remove_shared_edges
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Remove all shared (overlapping) portions between edges.
Returns only the non-shared segments.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: segment_crossing
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Find the crossing point of two non-collinear line segments.
Returns (intersection_point, t_on_a, u_on_b), or None.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: split_at_crossings
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Split all edges at crossing intersection points.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: turn_angle
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Signed turn angle from direction d_fwd to d_out.
In y-down coords: negative = left turn (CCW), positive = right turn (CW).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: walk_cw_loops
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Walk the directed segment graph clockwise, using the leftmost-turn rule
at junctions to trace union boundaries.

At each junction (where multiple outgoing edges exist), picks the
most-left (most CCW / most negative turn angle in y-down) outgoing edge.
This traces the outer boundary of overlapping polygon unions.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: remove_collinear_points
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Remove collinear midpoints from a closed polygon loop.
If three consecutive points are collinear, the middle one is redundant.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: filter_interior_loops
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Remove loops whose centroid lies inside a larger loop.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: polygon_area
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Absolute area of a polygon (shoelace formula).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: polygon_centroid
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Centroid of a polygon (average of vertices).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: point_in_polygon
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment: Point-in-polygon test using ray casting.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: generate_polygon_layout
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Generate the polygon-based layout.

1. Collect all edges from all polygons.
2. Remove shared edges (any segment that overlaps with another).
3. Chain surviving segments into closed loops.
4. Emit each loop as a closed-loop DD wall.
5. Register each input polygon as a DD shape for interior fill.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: point_on_segment
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Check if a point lies on a line segment. Returns the parametric t value
(0.0 = start, 1.0 = end) if the point is on the segment, None otherwise.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: edge_orientation
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Compute portal rotation and direction for an edge.

DD portals use `rotation` as the angle of the wall edge (not the normal).
The `direction` vector is `(cos(rotation), sin(rotation))`.
- Horizontal wall (E-W): rotation = 0, direction = (1, 0)
- Vertical wall (N-S): rotation = π/2, direction = (0, 1)

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.name: generate_portals
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.signature:
(
    polygons: &[PolygonConfig],
    loops: &[Vec<[f64; 2]>],
    wall_ids: &[String],
    alloc: &NodeIdAllocator,
) -> (Vec<MapPortal>, Vec<(usize, MapPortal)>)
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/polygons.rs.doc_comment:
Generate portals for all polygons after wall loop computation.

For each portal config on a polygon:
1. Compute its pixel position on the original edge.
2. Search the merged loops for a segment containing that point.
3. If found → wall-anchored portal (embedded in the wall).
4. If not found → freestanding portal (shared edge was removed).

Corridors generate parallel wall segments connecting rooms.
Returns walls, portals, shapes data, and terrain overrides.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.name: generate_terrain
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.signature:
(
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    width: u32,
    height: u32,
    config: &TerrainConfig,
) -> Terrain
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.doc_comment:
Generate terrain from a noise map.

The splat map has 4x4 cells per grid square. Each cell has 4 bytes (RGBA),
one weight per texture slot, summing to 255.

# Arguments
* `noise_map` - Source noise (any resolution — will be sampled)
* `width` - Map width in grid squares
* `height` - Map height in grid squares
* `config` - Terrain slot configuration

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.name: apply_road_corridor
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.signature:
(
    terrain: &mut Terrain,
    width: u32,
    corridor: &[(f64, f64)],
    road_half_width: f64,
    road_slot: usize,
)
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.doc_comment:
Modify terrain splat data along a road corridor.

Overrides terrain weights within the corridor to use a specific slot
(typically slot 0 for dirt/road texture).

# Arguments
* `terrain` - Mutable terrain to modify
* `width` - Map width in grid squares
* `corridor` - List of (x, y) points defining the road center (in pixel space, 256px/grid)
* `road_half_width` - Half-width of the road in pixels
* `road_slot` - Which texture slot (0-3) to use for the road

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.name: compute_weights
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.doc_comment: Compute normalized RGBA weights for a noise value across 4 slots.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.name: slot_weight
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.signature: (noise_val: f64, lower: f64, upper: f64, blend_width: f64) -> f64
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/terrain.rs.doc_comment: Compute the raw weight for a single slot based on noise value.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: generate_water
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Generate water bodies from a noise map.

For radial modes (island/lake), uses radial sampling to produce a single
clean closed polygon. For natural noise, falls back to marching squares
contour extraction.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: generate_water_radial
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Generate water with radial sampling mode for lake presets.
Casts rays from the center outward to find the threshold boundary,
producing one clean closed polygon.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: generate_water_island
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment: Generate water for island-mode maps (water ring around edges).

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: WaterMode
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
outputting DD-compatible WaterTree structures.
Extracts water polygons from noise thresholds using Marching Squares,

Water body generation.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: generate_water_inner
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.signature:
(
    noise_map: &NoiseMap,
    config: &WaterConfig,
    alloc: &NodeIdAllocator,
    mode: WaterMode,
) -> Water
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
outputting DD-compatible WaterTree structures.
Extracts water polygons from noise thresholds using Marching Squares,

Water body generation.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: generate_island_water_tree
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Build island water tree: ocean rectangle parent → island shoreline child (transparent hole).

This matches the Dungeondraft convention: the parent polygon fills ocean with water colors,
and a child polygon with transparent colors ("00000000") cuts a hole for the island.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: radial_water_polygon
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a single closed water polygon by radial sampling from the map center.

For each angle (0..360), casts a ray outward from the center and finds where
the noise value drops below the threshold. This produces one clean closed polygon
suitable for lake-mode maps where water is in the center.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: island_shoreline
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Trace the island shoreline via radial sampling.

Casts rays from the map center outward, finding where noise exceeds the
water threshold. Returns a closed polygon of shoreline points in pixel coordinates.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: water_from_polygon
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Generate water from a river corridor polygon.

Takes the water polygon points from `RiverResult` and builds a WaterTree.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: water_from_river
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment: Build a WaterTree from a river polygon, using the river config's own colors.

crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.name: water_node_ref
crates/mimir-mapgen/src/water.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a water tree node ref in the large negative range that DD expects.
DD uses Godot node IDs which are large negative integers.

Shared context for the MCP server.

Contains database path and state that persists across tool calls.
Connections are created on-demand via `connect()`.

crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.name: McpContext
crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
This allows concurrent read operations with SQLite WAL mode.
Database connections are created on-demand rather than held in a mutex.

Manages database connections and shared state for the MCP server.

MCP Context

crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.name: new
crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new context from `MIMIR_DATABASE_PATH` environment variable.

The env var must point to the SQLite database file
(e.g. `~/Library/Application Support/com.mimir.app/data/mimir.db`).

crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.name: connect
crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new database connection.

Each connection is configured with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled.
Returns an error if the connection cannot be established.

crates/mimir-mcp/src/context.rs.name: for_testing

Create a context for testing with a temporary file-based database.

Uses a unique temp file so multiple connections share the same DB.
The database is initialized with migrations run.

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: McpResponse
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Helper for constructing standardized MCP tool responses.

All methods return `Result<Value, McpError>` for consistency with tool handlers.

# Response Patterns

- **List**: `{ "entity_plural": [...], "count": N }`
- **Get**: `{ "entity": {...} }`
- **Created**: `{ "status": "created", "entity": {...} }`
- **Updated**: `{ "status": "updated", "entity": {...} }`
- **Deleted**: `{ "status": "deleted", "id": "..." }`
- **Success**: `{ "status": "success", "data": {...} }`
- **Ok**: Raw data as-is

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: McpResponse
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
for AI consumers. Analogous to Tauri's `ApiResponse<T>`.
Standardized response wrappers for MCP tools, providing a consistent API contract

MCP Response Helpers

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: list
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for listing multiple entities.

# Arguments
* `key` - Plural entity key (e.g., "campaigns", "modules", "items")
* `items` - Vector of JSON values representing the entities

# Returns
`{ "key": [...], "count": N }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::list("campaigns", campaign_data)
// => { "campaigns": [...], "count": 5 }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: get
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for getting a single entity.

# Arguments
* `key` - Singular entity key (e.g., "campaign", "module", "item")
* `data` - JSON value representing the entity

# Returns
`{ "key": {...} }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::get("campaign", campaign_json)
// => { "campaign": {...} }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: created
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for creating a new entity.

# Arguments
* `key` - Singular entity key (e.g., "campaign", "module", "item")
* `data` - JSON value representing the created entity

# Returns
`{ "status": "created", "key": {...} }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::created("campaign", campaign_json)
// => { "status": "created", "campaign": {...} }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: updated
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for updating an existing entity.

# Arguments
* `key` - Singular entity key (e.g., "campaign", "module", "item")
* `data` - JSON value representing the updated entity

# Returns
`{ "status": "updated", "key": {...} }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::updated("campaign", campaign_json)
// => { "status": "updated", "campaign": {...} }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: deleted
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for deleting an entity.

# Arguments
* `id` - The ID of the deleted entity

# Returns
`{ "status": "deleted", "id": "..." }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::deleted("abc-123")
// => { "status": "deleted", "id": "abc-123" }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: success
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for generic success with additional data.

Use this for operations that don't fit the CRUD pattern,
such as setting active campaign, export/import, etc.

# Arguments
* `data` - JSON value with the success details

# Returns
`{ "status": "success", "data": {...} }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::success(json!({ "archive_path": "/path/to/file.tar.gz" }))
// => { "status": "success", "data": { "archive_path": "/path/to/file.tar.gz" } }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: ok
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for raw data (no wrapper).

Use sparingly - prefer structured responses for consistency.
Useful for complex nested structures that don't fit standard patterns.

# Arguments
* `data` - JSON value to return as-is

# Returns
The data value unchanged.

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: added
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for operations that add/attach something to a parent entity.

# Arguments
* `key` - Singular key for what was added (e.g., "monster", "token")
* `data` - JSON value representing the added item

# Returns
`{ "status": "added", "key": {...} }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::added("monster", monster_json)
// => { "status": "added", "monster": {...} }
```

crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.name: removed
crates/mimir-mcp/src/response.rs.doc_comment:
Response for operations that remove/detach something from a parent entity.

# Arguments
* `id` - The ID of the removed item

# Returns
`{ "status": "removed", "id": "..." }`

# Example
```ignore
McpResponse::removed("abc-123")
// => { "status": "removed", "id": "abc-123" }
```

crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.name: Renderable
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.signature: { fn to_typst(), fn toc_title(), fn page_break_before(), fn page_margin() }
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for anything that can be rendered to Typst markup

Implementors produce Typst markup strings that can be assembled
into a complete document by the `DocumentBuilder`.

crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.name: toc_title
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.doc_comment:
Title for table of contents entry

Return `Some(title)` to include this section in the TOC,
or `None` to exclude it.

crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.name: page_break_before
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.doc_comment:
Whether this section should start on a new page

Default is true for most sections.

crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.name: page_margin
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.doc_comment:
Custom page margin for this section (in inches)

Return `Some(margin)` to override the document default,
or `None` to use the document's configured margin.

crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.name: DocumentConfig
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.signature: { include_toc: bool, include_title_page: bool, page_numbers: bool, margin: f32, font_size: f32 }
crates/mimir-print/src/builder.rs.doc_comment: Configuration for document assembly

## Simple Template Rendering

# Usage

- **map_renderer**: Map image rendering with grid, LOS walls, and tokens
- **sections**: Renderable document sections (markdown, monsters, maps, etc.)
- **markdown**: Markdown to Typst conversion with frontmatter support
- **builder**: Composable DocumentBuilder for assembling multi-section documents
- **service**: High-level PrintService for template-based PDF generation
- **world**: Custom Typst World implementation for file/font resolution

The crate is organized into several layers:

# Architecture

using the Typst document compiler.
This crate provides print and PDF generation capabilities for Mimir,

mimir-print - PDF generation for Mimir using Typst

use mimir_print::PrintService;
```ignore

## Simple Template Rendering

# Usage

- **map_renderer**: Map image rendering with grid, LOS walls, and tokens
- **sections**: Renderable document sections (markdown, monsters, maps, etc.)
- **markdown**: Markdown to Typst conversion with frontmatter support
- **builder**: Composable DocumentBuilder for assembling multi-section documents
- **service**: High-level PrintService for template-based PDF generation
- **world**: Custom Typst World implementation for file/font resolution

The crate is organized into several layers:

# Architecture

using the Typst document compiler.
This crate provides print and PDF generation capabilities for Mimir,

crates/mimir-print/src/markdown.rs.name: markdown_to_typst
crates/mimir-print/src/markdown.rs.doc_comment:
Convert markdown text to Typst markup.

Handles:
- Headers (`#` -> `=`)
- Bold (`**text**` -> `*text*`)
- Italic (`*text*` -> `_text_`)
- Lists (preserved)
- Links (`[text](url)` -> `#link("url")[text]`)
- Code blocks (converted to raw blocks)
- Tables (converted to Typst table syntax)

crates/mimir-print/src/markdown.rs.name: escape_for_typst_string
crates/mimir-print/src/markdown.rs.doc_comment:
Escape text for use inside a Typst string literal.
Only backslashes and quotes need escaping inside strings.

crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.name: SMALL_CARD_DESC_BUDGET
crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.doc_comment:
Character budget for the description area on a 2.5" x 3.25" card.

At 5.5pt font on a ~2.3in text width, roughly 55 chars/line x 18 lines
≈ 990 chars. We use 800 as a conservative budget accounting for variable
header/stats height and the footer overlay.

crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.name: CardTextSplit
crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.signature: { front: String, back: String, is_foldable: bool }
crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.doc_comment: Result of splitting card text for foldable layout

crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.name: split_text_natural
crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.doc_comment:
Split text at a natural boundary near `budget` characters.

Boundary priority (searches backward from budget):
1. Paragraph break (double newline or ` - ` list item boundary)
2. Sentence end (`. ` or `.\n`)
3. Clause boundary (`, `)
4. Word boundary (last space)
5. Hard cut at budget (fallback)

crates/mimir-print/src/sections/card_uti.name: flatten_entries

list items, and other structured content.

Virtual files can be referenced in Typst source using their registered paths.

crates/mimir-print/src/world.rs.name: register_file

The path should be a simple name like "map_preview.png" which will be
accessible as "/_virtual/map_preview.png" in Typst source.

Strips the top-level directory prefix and writes directly to dest.

crates/mimir/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Application state shared across all Tauri commands.

This struct is managed by Tauri and can be accessed in command handlers
via the `State` extractor.

Database connections are created on-demand via `connect()` rather than
held in a mutex. This allows concurrent read operations with SQLite WAL mode.
